<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: # DOCTOR: UPDATE WORKFLOW (CONFIRM - PER-PHASE PROMPT)
3: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: role: Expert Operator running unified spec-kit update in interactive confirm mode
5: purpose: Execute dependency-safe database rebuild with explicit approval at every phase boundary.
6: action: flock -> prompt -> probe -> prompt -> migrate -> prompt -> snapshot -> dashboard -> dependency.order -> post.run.validation -> state.log
7: 
8: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: # OPERATING MODE
10: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: operating_mode:
12:   workflow: sequential_orchestrator
13:   compliance: MANDATORY
14:   execution: interactive
15:   approvals: per_phase_boundary
16:   tracking: phase_by_phase
17:   validation: post_run_gold_battery
18:   state_log: "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.last-run.json"
19: 
20: 
21: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: # UPDATE ORCHESTRATOR INVARIANT
23: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: update_orchestrator_invariant: |
25:   The update orchestrator is a single-instance, flock-protected database rebuild
26:   chain. It executes the dependency DAG in order, snapshots every in-scope DB
27:   before mutation unless explicitly waived, and restores failed plus downstream
28:   DBs on retry exhaustion. SIGINT follows ADR-001/ADR-007: set cancel, allow a
29:   ~5 sec SQLite settle window, restore the in-flight snapshot, log, and exit 130.
30:   Every abort path — build failure, snapshot failure, dependency failure, SIGINT,
31:   validation regression — MUST `rm -f .doctor-update.flock` before exit to prevent
32:   indefinite-lock leak (council Issue 2 sub-finding).
33: 
34: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: # UPSTREAM ASSETS
36: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37: upstream_assets:
38:   contract: "local command design contract"
39:   files:
40:     spec: "spec.md"  # REQ-004..REQ-017 orchestrator contract
41:     decision_record: "decision-record.md"  # ADR-001..ADR-008 safety decisions
42:     council_spec: "decision-record.md#adr-002"  # council 10-line /doctor:update spec family
43:     checklist: "checklist.md#doctor-update-orchestrator"  # CHK-502..CHK-511 acceptance bars
44:   pass_policy:
45:     flock_required: true  # ADR-005 / REQ-005
46:     full_snapshot_required: true  # ADR-002 / REQ-006 unless --no-snapshot
47:     retry_backoff_seconds: 5  # ADR-004
48:     sigint_settle_seconds: 5  # ADR-001 revises ADR-007 timing
49:     snapshot_retention_days: 30  # ADR-002 cleanup policy unless --keep-snapshots
50: 
51: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: # USER INPUTS
53: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54: user_inputs:
55:   mode: "confirm"
56:   force: "[FORCE] boolean"
57:   no_snapshot: "[NO_SNAPSHOT] boolean"
58:   cleanup_legacy: "[CLEANUP_LEGACY] boolean"
59:   migrate: "[MIGRATE] boolean"
60:   keep_snapshots: "[KEEP_SNAPSHOTS] boolean"
61:   resume_bootstrap: "[RESUME_BOOTSTRAP] boolean"
62:   skip_status_check: false
63: 
64: 
65: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66: # FIELD HANDLING
67: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68: field_handling:
69:   defaults:
70:     force_empty: "false"
71:     no_snapshot_empty: "false"
72:     cleanup_legacy_empty: "false"
73:     migrate_empty: "false"
74:     keep_snapshots_empty: "false"
75:     resume_bootstrap_empty: "false"
76:   force_policy:
77:     true: "warn on active MCP clients and continue"
78:     false: "warn and prompt/abort when active MCP clients are detected"
79:   no_snapshot_policy:
80:     true: "skip DB snapshots; rollback is unavailable for unsnapshotted DBs"
81:     false: "snapshot every allowed DB before dependency execution"
82:   migrate_policy:
83:     true: "run migration-manifest Phase 0 before snapshots"
84:     false: "skip migration phase unless version-skip is auto-detected"
85:   cleanup_legacy_policy:
86:     true: "detect known legacy files from manifest and prompt-delete each"
87:     false: "report legacy findings without deletion prompts"
88:   keep_snapshots_policy:
89:     true: "skip snapshot cleanup older than 30 days"
90:     false: "cleanup snapshots older than 30 days during final phase"
91:   resume_bootstrap_policy:
92:     true: "verify .doctor-update.bootstrap.json completed before continuing after a fresh OpenCode restart"
93:     false: "run non-MCP runtime bootstrap and stop with restart_required if bootstrap changed tool-registration inputs"
94: 
95: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
96: # MUTATION BOUNDARIES
97: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
98: mutation_boundaries:
99:   allowed_targets:
100:     - ".opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite"  # structural code graph DB (skill-local)
101:     - ".opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
102:     - "mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite"  # active profile memory/vector DB
103:     - "mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
104:     - ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite"  # standalone advisor routing graph DB
105:     - ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
106:     - ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite"  # research/review coverage graph DB
107:     - ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
108:     - "mcp_server/database/speckit-eval.db"  # eval/ablation DB
109:     - "mcp_server/database/speckit-eval.db.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
110:     - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.flock"  # single-instance lock
111:     - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.lock"
112:     - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.last-run.json"  # orchestrator state log
113:     - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.bootstrap.json"  # pre-MCP bootstrap state
114:     - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.bootstrap.lockdir"  # pre-MCP bootstrap lock
115:     - ".opencode/skill"  # compatibility symlink only
116:     - ".opencode/skill_legacy_backup_*"  # legacy layout backup before symlink bridge
117:     - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/**"  # generated MCP runtime output
118:     - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/**"  # generated migration helper output
119:   forbidden_targets:
120:     - "<active-spec-folder>/**/*.md"  # never write into spec packet docs from /doctor:update
121:     - "<active-spec-folder>/description.json"  # spec metadata belongs to memory-save tooling
122:     - "<active-spec-folder>/graph-metadata.json"  # graph metadata not an orchestrator DB target
123:     - ".opencode/skills/**/SKILL.md"  # never edit skill bodies
124:     - ".opencode/skills/**/graph-metadata.json"
125:     - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/**/*.ts"
126:     - ".opencode/agents/**/*.md"
127:     - ".opencode/commands/doctor/*.md"
128:     - ".opencode/commands/doctor/assets/doctor_*.yaml"
129:     - "mcp_server/database/migration-manifest.json"
130:     - "**/migration-manifest.json"
131:     - "source files indexed by code graph or Code Graph"
132:     - "direct SQL outside VACUUM INTO snapshot and snapshot restore"
133:   validator:
134:     name: canonical_path_validator
135:     rule: "validate allowed_targets and forbidden_targets before snapshot, mutation, rollback, and state-log write"
136: 
137: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138: # STATE LOG SCHEMA
139: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140: state_log_schema:
141:   command: "/doctor:update"
142:   mode: "confirm"
143:   start: "ISO-8601"
144:   end: "ISO-8601"
145:   duration_seconds: "number"
146:   steps:
147:     - name: "subsystem"
148:       phase: "number"
149:       start: "ISO-8601"
150:       end: "ISO-8601"
151:       exit: "0|1|130"
152:       snapshot_path: "string|null"
153:   final_status: "ok|failed|rolled_back|cancelled|unrollbackable|restart_required"
154: 
155: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156: # INTERACTIVE PROMPTS
157: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
158: interactive_prompts:
159:   phase_boundary_options: ["approve", "inspect details", "skip safe optional step", "abort"]
160:   failure_options: ["retry from start", "rollback snapshot and exit", "leave as-is (dangerous)"]
161:   regression_options: ["rollback", "accept regression with note", "inspect"]
162: 
163: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164: # DEPENDENCY ORDER
165: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
166: dependency:
167:   order: ["code-graph", "context-index", "causal-edges-init", "skill-graph", "advisor", "deep-loop-graph", "speckit-eval"]
168: 
169: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170: # WORKFLOW
171: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
172: workflow:
173:   phase_0_runtime_bootstrap:
174:     council_line: 0
175:     approval: "auto-acknowledge before database flock; this phase uses Bash/Node only, no MCP tools"
176:     purpose: "Resolve the fresh-start paradox: OpenCode can only register MCP tools at process startup, so missing dist/layout must be fixed before MCP-dependent phases run."
177:     launcher_contract:
178:       mcp_config_entrypoint: ".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"
179:       invariant: "MCP configs MUST point to the stable launcher, not directly to mcp_server/dist/context-server.js."
180:       launcher_behavior: "resolve singular/plural skill layout, build @spec-kit/mcp-server and @spec-kit/scripts when dist is missing, then exec the real MCP server"
181:     command_preflight:
182:       script: ".opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh"
183:       command: "bash .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh --root . --json"
184:       state_log: ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.bootstrap.json"
185:       no_mcp_tools_allowed: true
186:     resume_rule:
187:       if_resume_bootstrap_true: "require bootstrap state status=complete and generated artifacts present; continue to phase_1_flock_acquire"
188:       if_resume_bootstrap_false_and_restart_required_true: "write .doctor-update.last-run.json with final_status=restart_required, release any bootstrap lock, stop before database flock"
189:       restart_message: "BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE_RESTART_REQUIRED: restart OpenCode so MCP tools register, then rerun /doctor:update --resume-bootstrap"
190:       max_reentry_count: 1
191:     required_artifacts:
192:       - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"
193:       - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js"
194:       - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/generate-description.js"
195:     failure:
196:       emit_error: "runtime bootstrap failed before MCP registration; inspect .doctor-update.bootstrap.json"
197:       final_status: "failed"
198:       exit_code: 1
199: 
200:   phase_1_flock_acquire:
201:     council_line: 1
202:     approval: "prompt before acquiring lock"
203:     flock: "ACQUIRE flock at mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.flock; refuse if held"
204:     pid_file: "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.lock"
205:     stale_lock_rule: "refuse if PID-file stale-locked >2h"
206:     refusal_message: "holding PID + start timestamp"
207: 
208:   phase_1_5_mcp_server_bootability:
209:     council_line: 1
210:     approval: "auto-acknowledge in confirm workflow (short tier per markdown)"
211:     preflight_check: |
212:       stat .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js
213:     rationale: "Defense-in-depth after Phase 0 and the MCP launcher. MCP tools listed in /doctor:update allowed-tools cannot be invoked unless the MCP server is built and bootable."
214:     if_dist_missing:
215:       action: "run the maintained non-MCP bootstrap script, then stop for fresh OpenCode startup instead of continuing into MCP phases"
216:       commands: |
217:         bash .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh --root .
218:       timeout_seconds: 240
219:       on_build_failure:
220:         emit_error: "system-spec-kit runtime build failed; cannot proceed with database operations"
221:         release_flock: "rm -f .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.flock .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.lock"
222:         exit_code: 1
223:       on_build_success:
224:         final_status: "restart_required"
225:         emit_message: "BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE_RESTART_REQUIRED: restart OpenCode so MCP tools register, then rerun /doctor:update --resume-bootstrap"
226:     if_dist_present:
227:       action: "verify timestamp newer than 30 days OR mcp_server/lib/**/*.ts (catch stale builds)"
228:       stale_threshold_days: 30
229:       on_stale: "warn but proceed; recommend rebuild outside flock"
230:     flock_release_invariant: "On ANY abort path from this phase forward (failure, SIGINT, exception), release the flock file BEFORE exit. Each downstream phase inherits this invariant."
231: 
232:   phase_2_probe_mcp_activity:
233:     council_line: 2
234:     approval: "prompt before probing active clients"
235:     probe: "PROBE other MCP-client activity"
236:     active_behavior: "if active, warn and prompt; --force marks force-ok"
237: 
238:   phase_8_migration_phase_0:
239:     council_line: 8
240:     approval: "prompt before migration manifest Phase 0"
241:     condition: |
242:       user_inputs.migrate == true
243:       OR (
244:         file_system_signals_detected >= 2 of [
245:           (.opencode/skill exists AND is a directory NOT a symlink),
246:           (.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js does NOT exist),
247:           (find . -path './.opencode' -prune -o -name 'memory' -type d -print | grep -q .),
248:           (find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -name 'spec.md' | first-match dirname has no description.json)
249:         ]
250:       )
251:       ELSE refuse to proceed when version detection ambiguous and neither --migrate nor --no-migrate flag was passed.
252:     file_system_signals:
253:       signal_a:
254:         check: "[ -d .opencode/skill ] && [ ! -L .opencode/skill ]"
255:         meaning: "v3.3-era singular directory present as a real directory (not bridge symlink)"
256:       signal_b:
257:         check: "[ ! -f .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js ]"
258:         meaning: "MCP server dist missing — fresh tarball install or post-clone state"
259:       signal_c:
260:         check: "find .opencode/specs -name 'memory' -type d -print -quit 2>/dev/null | grep -q ."
261:         meaning: "legacy memory/ folders inside any spec packet (v3.3 continuity surface)"
262:       signal_d:
263:         check: |
264:           if [ -d .opencode/specs ]; then
265:             # FIX-12: removed `head -1` (was limiting check to FIRST folder only).
266:             # Now iterates ALL spec.md until grep -q hits the first "missing" emission and short-circuits.
267:             find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 5 -name 'spec.md' 2>/dev/null \
268:               | while IFS= read -r f; do
269:                 dir=$(dirname "$f")
270:                 [ ! -f "$dir/description.json" ] && echo missing
271:               done | grep -q missing
272:           else
273:             false
274:           fi
275:         meaning: "spec folders exist but lack description.json (v3.4-required metadata not backfilled from v3.3)"
276:     auto_migrate_threshold: 2
277:     rationale: "package.json version field is a build-time placeholder ('0.0.0' in release tarballs), so version detection MUST use file-system signals instead. Council FIX-03."
278:     migration: "run migration-manifest.json Phase 0 before snapshots"
279:     gap_detected: "refuse to proceed if manifest gap detected"
280:     directory_layout_bridge:
281:       precondition: "both .opencode/skill/ and .opencode/skills/ directories exist (legacy v3.3 layout + current v3.4 layout)"
282:       detect_command: |
283:         [ -d .opencode/skill ] && [ -d .opencode/skills ] && [ ! -L .opencode/skill ]
284:       bridge_action: |
285:         rm -rf .opencode/skill_legacy_backup_$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) 2>/dev/null
286:         mv .opencode/skill .opencode/skill_legacy_backup_$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)
287:         ln -sf skills .opencode/skill
288:       on_symlink_failure:
289:         action: "write per-file remediation instructions"
290:         instruction_file: ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.config-instructions"
291:         content_template: |
292:           # /doctor:update remediation: directory rename
293:           # Run these commands manually to update v3.3 -> v3.4 paths:
294:           sed -i.bak 's|\.opencode/skill/|.opencode/skills/|g' .mcp.json opencode.json 2>/dev/null
295:           # Then manually verify .mcp.json and opencode.json are correct.
296:       idempotency: "if .opencode/skill is already a symlink to skills, skip"
297:     spec_metadata_backfill:
298:       precondition: "signal_d positive (spec folders exist but lack description.json) OR signal_c positive (legacy memory/ folders detected)"
299:       rationale: "v3.3 spec packets lack the description.json + graph-metadata.json files that v3.4 requires for memory_index_scan visibility. Without backfill, Phase 5.2 silently skips all v3.3 packets — see Issue 11 in council Round 2 backlog."
300:       mutation_boundary_exception: "Phase 8 INVOKES the sanctioned backfill scripts (generate-description.js + backfill-graph-metadata.js). The orchestrator does not edit description.json/graph-metadata.json directly; it delegates to maintained tooling per migration-manifest M-3.3.0.0-002 fallback_operation."
301:       step_a_graph_metadata_backfill:
302:         command: |
303:           # FIX-13: --active-only skips z_archive/z_future paths that the script's path-validator
304:           # rejects with "Spec folder is not under a supported specs root" — those archive trees
305:           # are outside graph-metadata backfill's traversal contract. Note: this is separate from
306:           # the memory index scope. Per the archive-retention fix (commit b062b12b4), z_archive content IS
307:           # indexed into the memory DB and deprioritized via ARCHIVE_MULTIPLIERS 0.1; only
308:           # graph-metadata backfill's path-validator skips it.
309:           node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js --active-only 2>&1 \
310:             | tee .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.backfill-graph.log
311:         timeout_seconds: 300
312:         on_failure: "warn but proceed (Phase 5.2 indexing will be partial); record failure in state log"
313:       step_b_description_backfill:
314:         command: |
315:           # Loop over spec folders missing description.json
316:           missing_count=0
317:           created_count=0
318:           failed_count=0
319:           while read -r spec_md; do
320:             dir=$(dirname "$spec_md")
321:             if [ ! -f "$dir/description.json" ]; then
322:               missing_count=$((missing_count + 1))
323:               if node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/generate-description.js \
324:                 "$dir" "$(pwd)" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
325:                 created_count=$((created_count + 1))
326:               else
327:                 failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
328:               fi
329:             fi
330:           done < <(find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 5 -name 'spec.md' 2>/dev/null)
331:           echo "{\"missing\":$missing_count,\"created\":$created_count,\"failed\":$failed_count}" \
332:             > .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.backfill-description.log
333:         timeout_seconds: 600
334:         on_failure: "warn per-folder; aggregate failed_count for state log"
335:       report_to_dashboard: "Phase 4 dashboard adds row 'spec-metadata-backfill: created=N, failed=M' when this sub-action runs"
336:     legacy_memory_md_detection:
337:       precondition: "always run when Phase 8 is triggered"
338:       rationale: "v3.3 used memory/*.md files inside spec folders for continuity. v3.4 moved continuity to _memory.continuity frontmatter blocks in implementation-summary.md. The migration is NOT fully automatable — content review required per migration-manifest M-3.3.0.0-001 fallback_operation. Phase 8 detects + reports, the user runs /memory:save manually after review."
339:       detection: |
340:         find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 6 -path '*/memory/*.md' \
341:           -not -path '*/scratch/*' -not -path '*/z_archive/*' 2>/dev/null \
342:           | tee .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.legacy-memory-files.log \
343:           | wc -l
344:       action_on_findings:
345:         do_not_auto_migrate: "respecting manifest M-3.3.0.0-001 'review first' policy"
346:         write_summary_log: ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.legacy-memory-files.log"
347:         recommend_to_user: |
348:           # Emit at end of Phase 8:
349:           if [ "$LEGACY_MEMORY_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
350:             echo ""
351:             echo "/doctor:update detected $LEGACY_MEMORY_COUNT legacy memory/*.md files in spec packets."
352:             echo "These are v3.3 continuity artifacts that were superseded by _memory.continuity frontmatter in v3.4."
353:             echo ""
354:             echo "RECOMMENDED MANUAL STEP: review the files listed in .doctor-update.legacy-memory-files.log,"
355:             echo "then run \`/memory:save\` for each affected packet to migrate continuity into the v3.4 frontmatter format."
356:             echo ""
357:           fi
358: 
359:   phase_3_snapshot_all_databases:
360:     council_line: 3
361:     approval: "prompt before snapshot; include disk use estimate"
362:     condition: "unless --no-snapshot"
363:     snapshot: "SNAPSHOT every *.sqlite via VACUUM INTO"
364:     filename: "<name>.pre-doctor-update.<version>.<timestamp>.bak"
365:     disk_free: "refuse if disk free < 2x DB total"
366:     post_snapshot_hook: |
367:       # Preserve original mtime on snapshot copies so age-based recovery decisions stay accurate.
368:       # VACUUM INTO creates a new file with current mtime by default; reset to source mtime.
369:       for src in mcp_server/database/*.sqlite mcp_server/database/*.db; do
370:         [ -f "$src" ] || continue
371:         for bak in "$src".pre-doctor-update.*.bak; do
372:           [ -f "$bak" ] || continue
373:           touch -r "$src" "$bak"
374:         done
375:       done
376:     rationale: "VACUUM INTO sets mtime to the snapshot moment, not the source's last-modified time. For age-based recovery decisions, snapshot mtime should reflect the source state being checkpointed. Council FIX-05."
377: 
378:   phase_4_status_dashboard:
379:     council_line: 4
380:     approval: "prompt after rendering dashboard and before selecting actions"
381:     cross_subsystem_dashboard:
382:       rows: ["code-graph", "context-index", "causal-edges", "skill-graph", "advisor", "deep-loop", "eval"]
383:       columns: ["status", "age", "coverage", "recommended action"]
384:       coverage_column_rationale: "context-index row populated by FIX-10 post_run_count_skipped output; other rows show n/a unless their tool surfaces a coverage metric"
385: 
386:   phase_5_dependency_order_execute:
387:     council_line: 4
388:     approval: "prompt before each dependency step"
389:     dependency.order: ["code-graph", "context-index", "causal-edges-init", "skill-graph", "advisor", "deep-loop-graph", "speckit-eval"]
390:     test_failure_injection:
391:       env_var: "SPECKIT_FAIL_STEP"
392:       scope: "disposable workspace tests only"
393:       rule: "Before executing each dependency step, if SPECKIT_FAIL_STEP exactly equals the dependency step name, inject a synthetic failure after snapshots and before mutating that step."
394:       example: "SPECKIT_FAIL_STEP=causal-edges-init"
395:       state_log_requirement: "Record injected_failure=true, failed_step=<step>, and final_status=rolled_back when rollback is selected."
396:     execute:
397:       code-graph: "code_graph_scan({ incremental: false })"
398:       context-index:
399:         action: "memory_index_scan({ incremental: false, force: true })"
400:         post_run_count_skipped: |
401:           # Count spec folders that exist but were not indexed.
402:           # memory_index_scan silently skips folders missing description.json or graph-metadata.json.
403:           total_specs=$(find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 5 -name 'spec.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
404:           indexed_specs=$(node -e "
405:             const Database = require('better-sqlite3');
406:             const fs = require('fs');
407:             const path = require('path');
408:             const dir = '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database';
409:             // Picks the first context-index shard for the coverage count; when multiple provider DBs coexist this is ambiguous. Unambiguous active-profile selection (via shared/embeddings/profile.js resolveActiveProfileDbPath) is tracked as a follow-on code task, not wired here.
410:             const file = fs.readdirSync(dir).find((name) => /^context-index__.*\.sqlite$/.test(name));
411:             if (!file) {
412:               console.log(0);
413:               process.exit(0);
414:             }
415:             const db = new Database(path.join(dir, file));
416:             const r = db.prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT spec_folder) AS n FROM memory_index WHERE spec_folder IS NOT NULL\").get();
417:             console.log(r.n);
418:             db.close();
419:           " 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
420:           skipped=$((total_specs - indexed_specs))
421:           echo "{\"total_specs\":$total_specs,\"indexed\":$indexed_specs,\"skipped\":$skipped}" \
422:             > .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.context-index-coverage.log
423:         report_when_skipped: |
424:           if [ "$skipped" -gt 0 ]; then
425:             echo "WARNING: context-index coverage gap detected"
426:             echo "  total spec folders found: $total_specs"
427:             echo "  indexed:                  $indexed_specs"
428:             echo "  skipped:                  $skipped (likely missing description.json or graph-metadata.json)"
429:             echo "  run Phase 8 backfill OR /memory:save per affected packet to fix"
430:           fi
431:       causal-edges-init: "memory_causal_link(...) for high-confidence missing lineage"
432:       skill-graph: "mk_skill_advisor.skill_graph_scan({})"
433:       advisor: "mk_skill_advisor.advisor_rebuild({ force: true }) + mk_skill_advisor.advisor_validate({})"
434:       deep-loop-graph: "deep_loop_graph_upsert(...)"
435:       speckit-eval: "eval_run_ablation({})"
436:     failure:
437:       council_line: 5
438:       retry: "one retry with 5-sec backoff"
439:       after_retry_failure: "prompt retry / rollback / leave"
440: 
441:   phase_5_5_post_dependency_checkpoint:
442:     council_line: 5
443:     approval: "auto-acknowledge unless --keep-snapshots is set"
444:     optional: true
445:     condition: "any sqlite/db file exists now that did NOT exist when phase_3_snapshot_all_databases ran (typical: code-graph, context-index, etc. on a fresh install)"
446:     detect_command: |
447:       comm -13 \
448:         <(ls mcp_server/database/*.pre-doctor-update.*.bak 2>/dev/null | sed 's/\.pre-doctor-update\..*\.bak$//' | sort -u) \
449:         <(ls mcp_server/database/*.sqlite mcp_server/database/*.db 2>/dev/null | sort -u)
450:     checkpoint_action: |
451:       # For each newly-created DB, take a "post-bootstrap" checkpoint snapshot.
452:       for new_db in $(comm -13 ...); do
453:         sqlite3 "$new_db" ".backup '${new_db}.post-doctor-update.${VERSION}.${TIMESTAMP}.bak'" 2>/dev/null \
454:           || cp -p "$new_db" "${new_db}.post-doctor-update.${VERSION}.${TIMESTAMP}.bak"
455:       done
456:     rationale: "Phase 3 snapshots only EXISTING DBs. On fresh install, all DBs are created during Phase 5, leaving no rollback target if Phase 7 validation fails. Phase 5.5 fills this gap. Council FIX-06."
457: 
458:   phase_6_sigint_cancel_contract:
459:     council_line: 6
460:     SIGINT: "set cancel flag; let current SQLite tx commit/abort; restore snapshot of in-flight DB; log cancelled by user; exit 130"
461:     settle_window: "~5 sec per ADR-001"
462: 
463:   phase_7_post_run_validation:
464:     council_line: 7
465:     approval: "prompt before accepting validation results when any warning exists"
466:     post.run.validation:
467:       - "existence + schema-version + row-count delta within bounds"
468:       - "gold-battery sanity check per DB"
469:     regression: "prompt"
470: 
471:   phase_9_legacy_cleanup:
472:     council_line: 9
473:     condition: "user_inputs.cleanup_legacy == true"
474:     legacy: "detect known-legacy files per migration-manifest"
475:     deletion: "prompt-delete each; never silent"
476: 
477:   phase_10_state_log_unlock_cleanup:
478:     council_line: 10
479:     state.log: "write .doctor-update.last-run.json with timestamps, durations, snapshot paths"
480:     release_flock: true
481:     cleanup: "prompt before deleting snapshots >30 days unless --keep-snapshots"
482: 
483: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
484: # OUTPUT FORMAT
485: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
486: output_format: |
487:   Render phase decisions, cross-subsystem health dashboard, validation results, state log path, and STATUS.

(End of file - total 487 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR: UPDATE WORKFLOW (CONFIRM - PER-PHASE PROMPT)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running unified spec-kit update in interactive confirm mode
purpose: Execute dependency-safe database rebuild with explicit approval at every phase boundary.
action: flock -> prompt -> probe -> prompt -> migrate -> prompt -> snapshot -> dashboard -> dependency.order -> post.run.validation -> state.log

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential_orchestrator
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase_boundary
  tracking: phase_by_phase
  validation: post_run_gold_battery
  state_log: "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.last-run.json"

display.text:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR: UPDATE WORKFLOW (CONFIRM - PER-PHASE PROMPT)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running unified spec-kit update in interactive confirm mode
purpose: Execute dependency-safe database rebuild with explicit approval at every phase boundary.
action: flock -> prompt -> probe -> prompt -> migrate -> prompt -> snapshot -> dashboard -> dependency.order -> post.run.validation -> state.log

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential_orchestrator
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase_boundary
  tracking: phase_by_phase
  validation: post_run_gold_battery
  state_log: "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.last-run.json"


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# UPDATE ORCHESTRATOR INVARIANT
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
update_orchestrator_invariant: |
  The update orchestrator is a single-instance, flock-protected database rebuild
  chain. It executes the dependency DAG in order, snapshots every in-scope DB
  before mutation unless explicitly waived, and restores failed plus downstream
  DBs on retry exhaustion. SIGINT follows ADR-001/ADR-007: set cancel, allow a
  ~5 sec SQLite settle window, restore the in-flight snapshot, log, and exit 130.
  Every abort path — build failure, snapshot failure, dependency failure, SIGINT,
  validation regression — MUST `rm -f .doctor-update.flock` before exit to prevent
  indefinite-lock leak (council Issue 2 sub-finding).

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# UPSTREAM ASSETS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
upstream_assets:
  contract: "local command design contract"
  files:
    spec: "spec.md"  # REQ-004..REQ-017 orchestrator contract
    decision_record: "decision-record.md"  # ADR-001..ADR-008 safety decisions
    council_spec: "decision-record.md#adr-002"  # council 10-line /doctor:update spec family
    checklist: "checklist.md#doctor-update-orchestrator"  # CHK-502..CHK-511 acceptance bars
  pass_policy:
    flock_required: true  # ADR-005 / REQ-005
    full_snapshot_required: true  # ADR-002 / REQ-006 unless --no-snapshot
    retry_backoff_seconds: 5  # ADR-004
    sigint_settle_seconds: 5  # ADR-001 revises ADR-007 timing
    snapshot_retention_days: 30  # ADR-002 cleanup policy unless --keep-snapshots

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USER INPUTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_inputs:
  mode: "confirm"
  force: "[FORCE] boolean"
  no_snapshot: "[NO_SNAPSHOT] boolean"
  cleanup_legacy: "[CLEANUP_LEGACY] boolean"
  migrate: "[MIGRATE] boolean"
  keep_snapshots: "[KEEP_SNAPSHOTS] boolean"
  resume_bootstrap: "[RESUME_BOOTSTRAP] boolean"
  skip_status_check: false


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# FIELD HANDLING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
field_handling:
  defaults:
    force_empty: "false"
    no_snapshot_empty: "false"
    cleanup_legacy_empty: "false"
    migrate_empty: "false"
    keep_snapshots_empty: "false"
    resume_bootstrap_empty: "false"
  force_policy:
    true: "warn on active MCP clients and continue"
    false: "warn and prompt/abort when active MCP clients are detected"
  no_snapshot_policy:
    true: "skip DB snapshots; rollback is unavailable for unsnapshotted DBs"
    false: "snapshot every allowed DB before dependency execution"
  migrate_policy:
    true: "run migration-manifest Phase 0 before snapshots"
    false: "skip migration phase unless version-skip is auto-detected"
  cleanup_legacy_policy:
    true: "detect known legacy files from manifest and prompt-delete each"
    false: "report legacy findings without deletion prompts"
  keep_snapshots_policy:
    true: "skip snapshot cleanup older than 30 days"
    false: "cleanup snapshots older than 30 days during final phase"
  resume_bootstrap_policy:
    true: "verify .doctor-update.bootstrap.json completed before continuing after a fresh OpenCode restart"
    false: "run non-MCP runtime bootstrap and stop with restart_required if bootstrap changed tool-registration inputs"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MUTATION BOUNDARIES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mutation_boundaries:
  allowed_targets:
    - ".opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite"  # structural code graph DB (skill-local)
    - ".opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
    - "mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite"  # active profile memory/vector DB
    - "mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
    - ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite"  # standalone advisor routing graph DB
    - ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
    - ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite"  # research/review coverage graph DB
    - ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
    - "mcp_server/database/speckit-eval.db"  # eval/ablation DB
    - "mcp_server/database/speckit-eval.db.pre-doctor-update.*.bak"
    - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.flock"  # single-instance lock
    - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.lock"
    - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.last-run.json"  # orchestrator state log
    - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.bootstrap.json"  # pre-MCP bootstrap state
    - "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.bootstrap.lockdir"  # pre-MCP bootstrap lock
    - ".opencode/skill"  # compatibility symlink only
    - ".opencode/skill_legacy_backup_*"  # legacy layout backup before symlink bridge
    - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/**"  # generated MCP runtime output
    - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/**"  # generated migration helper output
  forbidden_targets:
    - "<active-spec-folder>/**/*.md"  # never write into spec packet docs from /doctor:update
    - "<active-spec-folder>/description.json"  # spec metadata belongs to memory-save tooling
    - "<active-spec-folder>/graph-metadata.json"  # graph metadata not an orchestrator DB target
    - ".opencode/skills/**/SKILL.md"  # never edit skill bodies
    - ".opencode/skills/**/graph-metadata.json"
    - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/**/*.ts"
    - ".opencode/agents/**/*.md"
    - ".opencode/commands/doctor/*.md"
    - ".opencode/commands/doctor/assets/doctor_*.yaml"
    - "mcp_server/database/migration-manifest.json"
    - "**/migration-manifest.json"
    - "source files indexed by code graph or Code Graph"
    - "direct SQL outside VACUUM INTO snapshot and snapshot restore"
  validator:
    name: canonical_path_validator
    rule: "validate allowed_targets and forbidden_targets before snapshot, mutation, rollback, and state-log write"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STATE LOG SCHEMA
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
state_log_schema:
  command: "/doctor:update"
  mode: "confirm"
  start: "ISO-8601"
  end: "ISO-8601"
  duration_seconds: "number"
  steps:
    - name: "subsystem"
      phase: "number"
      start: "ISO-8601"
      end: "ISO-8601"
      exit: "0|1|130"
      snapshot_path: "string|null"
  final_status: "ok|failed|rolled_back|cancelled|unrollbackable|restart_required"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# INTERACTIVE PROMPTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
interactive_prompts:
  phase_boundary_options: ["approve", "inspect details", "skip safe optional step", "abort"]
  failure_options: ["retry from start", "rollback snapshot and exit", "leave as-is (dangerous)"]
  regression_options: ["rollback", "accept regression with note", "inspect"]

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEPENDENCY ORDER
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dependency:
  order: ["code-graph", "context-index", "causal-edges-init", "skill-graph", "advisor", "deep-loop-graph", "speckit-eval"]

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# WORKFLOW
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
workflow:
  phase_0_runtime_bootstrap:
    council_line: 0
    approval: "auto-acknowledge before database flock; this phase uses Bash/Node only, no MCP tools"
    purpose: "Resolve the fresh-start paradox: OpenCode can only register MCP tools at process startup, so missing dist/layout must be fixed before MCP-dependent phases run."
    launcher_contract:
      mcp_config_entrypoint: ".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"
      invariant: "MCP configs MUST point to the stable launcher, not directly to mcp_server/dist/context-server.js."
      launcher_behavior: "resolve singular/plural skill layout, build @spec-kit/mcp-server and @spec-kit/scripts when dist is missing, then exec the real MCP server"
    command_preflight:
      script: ".opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh"
      command: "bash .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh --root . --json"
      state_log: ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.bootstrap.json"
      no_mcp_tools_allowed: true
    resume_rule:
      if_resume_bootstrap_true: "require bootstrap state status=complete and generated artifacts present; continue to phase_1_flock_acquire"
      if_resume_bootstrap_false_and_restart_required_true: "write .doctor-update.last-run.json with final_status=restart_required, release any bootstrap lock, stop before database flock"
      restart_message: "BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE_RESTART_REQUIRED: restart OpenCode so MCP tools register, then rerun /doctor:update --resume-bootstrap"
      max_reentry_count: 1
    required_artifacts:
      - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"
      - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js"
      - ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/generate-description.js"
    failure:
      emit_error: "runtime bootstrap failed before MCP registration; inspect .doctor-update.bootstrap.json"
      final_status: "failed"
      exit_code: 1

  phase_1_flock_acquire:
    council_line: 1
    approval: "prompt before acquiring lock"
    flock: "ACQUIRE flock at mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.flock; refuse if held"
    pid_file: "mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.lock"
    stale_lock_rule: "refuse if PID-file stale-locked >2h"
    refusal_message: "holding PID + start timestamp"

  phase_1_5_mcp_server_bootability:
    council_line: 1
    approval: "auto-acknowledge in confirm workflow (short tier per markdown)"
    preflight_check: |
      stat .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js
    rationale: "Defense-in-depth after Phase 0 and the MCP launcher. MCP tools listed in /doctor:update allowed-tools cannot be invoked unless the MCP server is built and bootable."
    if_dist_missing:
      action: "run the maintained non-MCP bootstrap script, then stop for fresh OpenCode startup instead of continuing into MCP phases"
      commands: |
        bash .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh --root .
      timeout_seconds: 240
      on_build_failure:
        emit_error: "system-spec-kit runtime build failed; cannot proceed with database operations"
        release_flock: "rm -f .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.flock .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.lock"
        exit_code: 1
      on_build_success:
        final_status: "restart_required"
        emit_message: "BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE_RESTART_REQUIRED: restart OpenCode so MCP tools register, then rerun /doctor:update --resume-bootstrap"
    if_dist_present:
      action: "verify timestamp newer than 30 days OR mcp_server/lib/**/*.ts (catch stale builds)"
      stale_threshold_days: 30
      on_stale: "warn but proceed; recommend rebuild outside flock"
    flock_release_invariant: "On ANY abort path from this phase forward (failure, SIGINT, exception), release the flock file BEFORE exit. Each downstream phase inherits this invariant."

  phase_2_probe_mcp_activity:
    council_line: 2
    approval: "prompt before probing active clients"
    probe: "PROBE other MCP-client activity"
    active_behavior: "if active, warn and prompt; --force marks force-ok"

  phase_8_migration_phase_0:
    council_line: 8
    approval: "prompt before migration manifest Phase 0"
    condition: |
      user_inputs.migrate == true
      OR (
        file_system_signals_detected >= 2 of [
          (.opencode/skill exists AND is a directory NOT a symlink),
          (.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js does NOT exist),
          (find . -path './.opencode' -prune -o -name 'memory' -type d -print | grep -q .),
          (find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -name 'spec.md' | first-match dirname has no description.json)
        ]
      )
      ELSE refuse to proceed when version detection ambiguous and neither --migrate nor --no-migrate flag was passed.
    file_system_signals:
      signal_a:
        check: "[ -d .opencode/skill ] && [ ! -L .opencode/skill ]"
        meaning: "v3.3-era singular directory present as a real directory (not bridge symlink)"
      signal_b:
        check: "[ ! -f .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js ]"
        meaning: "MCP server dist missing — fresh tarball install or post-clone state"
      signal_c:
        check: "find .opencode/specs -name 'memory' -type d -print -quit 2>/dev/null | grep -q ."
        meaning: "legacy memory/ folders inside any spec packet (v3.3 continuity surface)"
      signal_d:
        check: |
          if [ -d .opencode/specs ]; then
            # FIX-12: removed `head -1` (was limiting check to FIRST folder only).
            # Now iterates ALL spec.md until grep -q hits the first "missing" emission and short-circuits.
            find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 5 -name 'spec.md' 2>/dev/null \
              | while IFS= read -r f; do
                dir=$(dirname "$f")
                [ ! -f "$dir/description.json" ] && echo missing
              done | grep -q missing
          else
            false
          fi
        meaning: "spec folders exist but lack description.json (v3.4-required metadata not backfilled from v3.3)"
    auto_migrate_threshold: 2
    rationale: "package.json version field is a build-time placeholder ('0.0.0' in release tarballs), so version detection MUST use file-system signals instead. Council FIX-03."
    migration: "run migration-manifest.json Phase 0 before snapshots"
    gap_detected: "refuse to proceed if manifest gap detected"
    directory_layout_bridge:
      precondition: "both .opencode/skill/ and .opencode/skills/ directories exist (legacy v3.3 layout + current v3.4 layout)"
      detect_command: |
        [ -d .opencode/skill ] && [ -d .opencode/skills ] && [ ! -L .opencode/skill ]
      bridge_action: |
        rm -rf .opencode/skill_legacy_backup_$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) 2>/dev/null
        mv .opencode/skill .opencode/skill_legacy_backup_$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)
        ln -sf skills .opencode/skill
      on_symlink_failure:
        action: "write per-file remediation instructions"
        instruction_file: ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.config-instructions"
        content_template: |
          # /doctor:update remediation: directory rename
          # Run these commands manually to update v3.3 -> v3.4 paths:
          sed -i.bak 's|\.opencode/skill/|.opencode/skills/|g' .mcp.json opencode.json 2>/dev/null
          # Then manually verify .mcp.json and opencode.json are correct.
      idempotency: "if .opencode/skill is already a symlink to skills, skip"
    spec_metadata_backfill:
      precondition: "signal_d positive (spec folders exist but lack description.json) OR signal_c positive (legacy memory/ folders detected)"
      rationale: "v3.3 spec packets lack the description.json + graph-metadata.json files that v3.4 requires for memory_index_scan visibility. Without backfill, Phase 5.2 silently skips all v3.3 packets — see Issue 11 in council Round 2 backlog."
      mutation_boundary_exception: "Phase 8 INVOKES the sanctioned backfill scripts (generate-description.js + backfill-graph-metadata.js). The orchestrator does not edit description.json/graph-metadata.json directly; it delegates to maintained tooling per migration-manifest M-3.3.0.0-002 fallback_operation."
      step_a_graph_metadata_backfill:
        command: |
          # FIX-13: --active-only skips z_archive/z_future paths that the script's path-validator
          # rejects with "Spec folder is not under a supported specs root" — those archive trees
          # are outside graph-metadata backfill's traversal contract. Note: this is separate from
          # the memory index scope. Per the archive-retention fix (commit b062b12b4), z_archive content IS
          # indexed into the memory DB and deprioritized via ARCHIVE_MULTIPLIERS 0.1; only
          # graph-metadata backfill's path-validator skips it.
          node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js --active-only 2>&1 \
            | tee .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.backfill-graph.log
        timeout_seconds: 300
        on_failure: "warn but proceed (Phase 5.2 indexing will be partial); record failure in state log"
      step_b_description_backfill:
        command: |
          # Loop over spec folders missing description.json
          missing_count=0
          created_count=0
          failed_count=0
          while read -r spec_md; do
            dir=$(dirname "$spec_md")
            if [ ! -f "$dir/description.json" ]; then
              missing_count=$((missing_count + 1))
              if node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/generate-description.js \
                "$dir" "$(pwd)" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                created_count=$((created_count + 1))
              else
                failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
              fi
            fi
          done < <(find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 5 -name 'spec.md' 2>/dev/null)
          echo "{\"missing\":$missing_count,\"created\":$created_count,\"failed\":$failed_count}" \
            > .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.backfill-description.log
        timeout_seconds: 600
        on_failure: "warn per-folder; aggregate failed_count for state log"
      report_to_dashboard: "Phase 4 dashboard adds row 'spec-metadata-backfill: created=N, failed=M' when this sub-action runs"
    legacy_memory_md_detection:
      precondition: "always run when Phase 8 is triggered"
      rationale: "v3.3 used memory/*.md files inside spec folders for continuity. v3.4 moved continuity to _memory.continuity frontmatter blocks in implementation-summary.md. The migration is NOT fully automatable — content review required per migration-manifest M-3.3.0.0-001 fallback_operation. Phase 8 detects + reports, the user runs /memory:save manually after review."
      detection: |
        find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 6 -path '*/memory/*.md' \
          -not -path '*/scratch/*' -not -path '*/z_archive/*' 2>/dev/null \
          | tee .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.legacy-memory-files.log \
          | wc -l
      action_on_findings:
        do_not_auto_migrate: "respecting manifest M-3.3.0.0-001 'review first' policy"
        write_summary_log: ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.legacy-memory-files.log"
        recommend_to_user: |
          # Emit at end of Phase 8:
          if [ "$LEGACY_MEMORY_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "/doctor:update detected $LEGACY_MEMORY_COUNT legacy memory/*.md files in spec packets."
            echo "These are v3.3 continuity artifacts that were superseded by _memory.continuity frontmatter in v3.4."
            echo ""
            echo "RECOMMENDED MANUAL STEP: review the files listed in .doctor-update.legacy-memory-files.log,"
            echo "then run \`/memory:save\` for each affected packet to migrate continuity into the v3.4 frontmatter format."
            echo ""
          fi

  phase_3_snapshot_all_databases:
    council_line: 3
    approval: "prompt before snapshot; include disk use estimate"
    condition: "unless --no-snapshot"
    snapshot: "SNAPSHOT every *.sqlite via VACUUM INTO"
    filename: "<name>.pre-doctor-update.<version>.<timestamp>.bak"
    disk_free: "refuse if disk free < 2x DB total"
    post_snapshot_hook: |
      # Preserve original mtime on snapshot copies so age-based recovery decisions stay accurate.
      # VACUUM INTO creates a new file with current mtime by default; reset to source mtime.
      for src in mcp_server/database/*.sqlite mcp_server/database/*.db; do
        [ -f "$src" ] || continue
        for bak in "$src".pre-doctor-update.*.bak; do
          [ -f "$bak" ] || continue
          touch -r "$src" "$bak"
        done
      done
    rationale: "VACUUM INTO sets mtime to the snapshot moment, not the source's last-modified time. For age-based recovery decisions, snapshot mtime should reflect the source state being checkpointed. Council FIX-05."

  phase_4_status_dashboard:
    council_line: 4
    approval: "prompt after rendering dashboard and before selecting actions"
    cross_subsystem_dashboard:
      rows: ["code-graph", "context-index", "causal-edges", "skill-graph", "advisor", "deep-loop", "eval"]
      columns: ["status", "age", "coverage", "recommended action"]
      coverage_column_rationale: "context-index row populated by FIX-10 post_run_count_skipped output; other rows show n/a unless their tool surfaces a coverage metric"

  phase_5_dependency_order_execute:
    council_line: 4
    approval: "prompt before each dependency step"
    dependency.order: ["code-graph", "context-index", "causal-edges-init", "skill-graph", "advisor", "deep-loop-graph", "speckit-eval"]
    test_failure_injection:
      env_var: "SPECKIT_FAIL_STEP"
      scope: "disposable workspace tests only"
      rule: "Before executing each dependency step, if SPECKIT_FAIL_STEP exactly equals the dependency step name, inject a synthetic failure after snapshots and before mutating that step."
      example: "SPECKIT_FAIL_STEP=causal-edges-init"
      state_log_requirement: "Record injected_failure=true, failed_step=<step>, and final_status=rolled_back when rollback is selected."
    execute:
      code-graph: "code_graph_scan({ incremental: false })"
      context-index:
        action: "memory_index_scan({ incremental: false, force: true })"
        post_run_count_skipped: |
          # Count spec folders that exist but were not indexed.
          # memory_index_scan silently skips folders missing description.json or graph-metadata.json.
          total_specs=$(find .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 5 -name 'spec.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
          indexed_specs=$(node -e "
            const Database = require('better-sqlite3');
            const fs = require('fs');
            const path = require('path');
            const dir = '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database';
            // Picks the first context-index shard for the coverage count; when multiple provider DBs coexist this is ambiguous. Unambiguous active-profile selection (via shared/embeddings/profile.js resolveActiveProfileDbPath) is tracked as a follow-on code task, not wired here.
            const file = fs.readdirSync(dir).find((name) => /^context-index__.*\.sqlite$/.test(name));
            if (!file) {
              console.log(0);
              process.exit(0);
            }
            const db = new Database(path.join(dir, file));
            const r = db.prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT spec_folder) AS n FROM memory_index WHERE spec_folder IS NOT NULL\").get();
            console.log(r.n);
            db.close();
          " 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          skipped=$((total_specs - indexed_specs))
          echo "{\"total_specs\":$total_specs,\"indexed\":$indexed_specs,\"skipped\":$skipped}" \
            > .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.context-index-coverage.log
        report_when_skipped: |
          if [ "$skipped" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: context-index coverage gap detected"
            echo "  total spec folders found: $total_specs"
            echo "  indexed:                  $indexed_specs"
            echo "  skipped:                  $skipped (likely missing description.json or graph-metadata.json)"
            echo "  run Phase 8 backfill OR /memory:save per affected packet to fix"
          fi
      causal-edges-init: "memory_causal_link(...) for high-confidence missing lineage"
      skill-graph: "mk_skill_advisor.skill_graph_scan({})"
      advisor: "mk_skill_advisor.advisor_rebuild({ force: true }) + mk_skill_advisor.advisor_validate({})"
      deep-loop-graph: "deep_loop_graph_upsert(...)"
      speckit-eval: "eval_run_ablation({})"
    failure:
      council_line: 5
      retry: "one retry with 5-sec backoff"
      after_retry_failure: "prompt retry / rollback / leave"

  phase_5_5_post_dependency_checkpoint:
    council_line: 5
    approval: "auto-acknowledge unless --keep-snapshots is set"
    optional: true
    condition: "any sqlite/db file exists now that did NOT exist when phase_3_snapshot_all_databases ran (typical: code-graph, context-index, etc. on a fresh install)"
    detect_command: |
      comm -13 \
        <(ls mcp_server/database/*.pre-doctor-update.*.bak 2>/dev/null | sed 's/\.pre-doctor-update\..*\.bak$//' | sort -u) \
        <(ls mcp_server/database/*.sqlite mcp_server/database/*.db 2>/dev/null | sort -u)
    checkpoint_action: |
      # For each newly-created DB, take a "post-bootstrap" checkpoint snapshot.
      for new_db in $(comm -13 ...); do
        sqlite3 "$new_db" ".backup '${new_db}.post-doctor-update.${VERSION}.${TIMESTAMP}.bak'" 2>/dev/null \
          || cp -p "$new_db" "${new_db}.post-doctor-update.${VERSION}.${TIMESTAMP}.bak"
      done
    rationale: "Phase 3 snapshots only EXISTING DBs. On fresh install, all DBs are created during Phase 5, leaving no rollback target if Phase 7 validation fails. Phase 5.5 fills this gap. Council FIX-06."

  phase_6_sigint_cancel_contract:
    council_line: 6
    SIGINT: "set cancel flag; let current SQLite tx commit/abort; restore snapshot of in-flight DB; log cancelled by user; exit 130"
    settle_window: "~5 sec per ADR-001"

  phase_7_post_run_validation:
    council_line: 7
    approval: "prompt before accepting validation results when any warning exists"
    post.run.validation:
      - "existence + schema-version + row-count delta within bounds"
      - "gold-battery sanity check per DB"
    regression: "prompt"

  phase_9_legacy_cleanup:
    council_line: 9
    condition: "user_inputs.cleanup_legacy == true"
    legacy: "detect known-legacy files per migration-manifest"
    deletion: "prompt-delete each; never silent"

  phase_10_state_log_unlock_cleanup:
    council_line: 10
    state.log: "write .doctor-update.last-run.json with timestamps, durations, snapshot paths"
    release_flock: true
    cleanup: "prompt before deleting snapshots >30 days unless --keep-snapshots"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OUTPUT FORMAT
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
output_format: |
  Render phase decisions, cross-subsystem health dashboard, validation results, state log path, and STATUS.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR: MEMORY CONTINUITY-INDEX WORKFLOW (INTERACTIVE DIAGNOSTIC MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running memory continuity-index diagnostics in interactive mode
purpose: Read-only health check + drift detection with user review gates. No mutations. Report goes to packet-local scratch.
action: Discovery → gate → analysis → gate → drift classification → recommendation → state log

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase
  tracking: phase_by_phase
  validation: continuous_self_validation

display.text:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR: MEMORY CONTINUITY-INDEX WORKFLOW (INTERACTIVE DIAGNOSTIC MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running memory continuity-index diagnostics in interactive mode
purpose: Read-only health check + drift detection with user review gates. No mutations. Report goes to packet-local scratch.
action: Discovery → gate → analysis → gate → drift classification → recommendation → state log

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase
  tracking: phase_by_phase
  validation: continuous_self_validation
  intent: DIAGNOSE  # never APPLY in this YAML


memory_doctor_invariant: |
  Memory doctor is read-only by contract. It treats the active provider-keyed
  profile DBs (ollama, hf-local, voyage, and openai profiles matching
  context-index__*.sqlite) as the memory-owned stores it diagnoses but never
  mutates. Rebuilds are owned by the /doctor:update orchestrator, which relies on
  the per-file memory_index_scan transaction model so SIGINT uses the ~5 sec settle
  window before restoring snapshots for cross-file consistency.

upstream_assets:
  contract: "local command design contract"
  cli_health_command: "node .opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs memory_health --format json --timeout-ms 500 --warm-only"
  cli_health_policy: "Warm-only: probe the existing mk-spec-memory IPC socket first. The --warm-only flag guarantees the CLI never starts a daemon; if the socket is absent or dead it exits 75 (backend unavailable) — report CLI availability as retryable-unavailable."
  files:
    spec: "spec.md"  # REQ-005..REQ-007 cover flock, snapshots, dashboard
    decision_record: "decision-record.md"  # ADR-001 documents memory_index_scan tx model
    tx_model: "decision-record.md#adr-001"  # per-file transactions + ~5 sec SIGINT settle
    checklist: "checklist.md#doctor-memory"  # CHK-104 gold-battery acceptance bar
  pass_policy:
    minimum_result_count_per_query: 1  # existing memory gold-battery floor
    post_result_count_floor_ratio: 0.80  # preserve current workflow threshold
    recall_at_20_floor_ratio: 0.80  # preserve current Recall@20 proxy
memory_doctor_philosophy:
  principle: "Detect drift before it breaks memory_search; this command never mutates — rebuilds run through /doctor:update"
  approach: "Read memory_health + memory_stats → classify drift signals → emit recommendations"
  mandate: "This command is read-only by contract."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USER INPUTS (passed from Markdown setup phase)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_inputs:
  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - INTERACTIVE (this YAML)"
  intent: "[INTENT] - DIAGNOSE (this YAML)"
  incremental: "[INCREMENTAL] - true|false (informational; no rebuild in this command)"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MUTATION BOUNDARIES (READ-ONLY)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

field_handling:
  defaults:
    incremental_empty: "false"
  incremental_policy:
    true: "diagnostic input only; recommend incremental apply when medium drift is detected"
    false: "diagnostic input only; recommend full apply when high-severity drift is detected"
mutation_boundaries:
  allowed_targets:
    - "mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite"  # provider-keyed active profile DBs
    - "mcp_server/database/context-index*.sqlite.pre-doctor-memory.*.bak"  # memory snapshot files only
  forbidden_targets:
    - "<active-spec-folder>/**/*.md"  # never write into spec packet docs from /doctor memory
    - "<active-spec-folder>/description.json"  # spec metadata belongs to memory-save tooling
    - "<active-spec-folder>/graph-metadata.json"  # graph metadata is not a memory doctor target
    - ".opencode/skills/**/SKILL.md"  # never edit skill bodies
    - ".opencode/skills/**/graph-metadata.json"
    - ".opencode/agents/**/*.md"
    - ".opencode/commands/doctor/*.md"
    - ".opencode/commands/doctor/assets/doctor_*.yaml"
    - "mcp_server/database/*code-graph*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*deep-loop*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*eval*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*skill*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*causal*"
  enforcement: "This command is READ-ONLY by contract. No mutation phases exist in this YAML. Any attempted write halts with STATUS=FAIL and ERROR='confirm-mode-mutation-violation'."
  validator:
    name: validate_targets
    runs: "before any file write; diagnostic mode should never reach a write path"
    steps:
      - "For each target: resolve via realpath; reject absolute paths; reject paths containing `..` or symlinks escaping repo root"
      - "Assert resolved target is repo-relative under repo_root"
      - "Match resolved target against allowed_targets patterns only"
      - "Reject any match against forbidden_targets patterns"
      - "On first violation: abort, append violation to state log, exit STATUS=FAIL with diagnostic"
    on_pass: "diagnostic reads may continue"
    on_fail: "abort before any write; STATUS=FAIL ERROR=\"target validation failed: <path>\""

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# INTERACTIVE GATES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
interactive_gates:
  before_phase_1_analysis:
    prompt: "Phase 0 complete. Continue to Phase 1? (y/n/details)"
    blocking: true
    approvals:
      y: "continue to phase_1_analysis"
      n: "STATUS=CANCELLED; emit state log with phase=phase_0_discovery"
      details: "print Phase 0 discovery outputs, then ask again"
  before_phase_2_recommendation:
    prompt: "Phase 1 complete. Continue to Phase 2? (y/n/details)"
    blocking: true
    approvals:
      y: "continue to phase_2_recommendation"
      n: "STATUS=CANCELLED; emit state log with phase=phase_1_analysis"
      details: "print Phase 1 drift classification outputs, then ask again"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STALENESS SIGNALS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
staleness_signals:
  embedding_drift:
    detection: "memory_health reports `embedding_health: degraded` OR records with `last_indexed_at` older than source file mtime"
    severity: medium
  fts_miss:
    detection: "memory_health reports `fts_health: degraded` OR new spec-doc files not in FTS index"
    severity: medium
  chunk_boundary_drift:
    detection: "Anchors moved or sections renumbered since last index"
    severity: low
  trigger_phrase_drift:
    detection: "Frontmatter trigger_phrases updated but indexed alias rows stale"
    severity: low
  embedding_provider_change:
    detection: "Vector DB rows with old provider id (voyage-2 vs voyage-4)"
    severity: high
  divergent_alias_groups:
    detection: "Same canonical name routed to different record IDs"
    severity: high
  hard_exclusion_risk:
    detection: "memory_health data.exclusionAudit diagnostics include hard-exclusion-risk or exclusion-unclassified"
    severity: high

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# WORKFLOW
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
workflow:
  phase_0_discovery:
    purpose: Probe memory_health + memory_stats; capture baseline
    activities:
      - "Apply upstream_assets.cli_health_policy, then run upstream_assets.cli_health_command only when the warm socket probe succeeds; report CLI availability, freshness, trustState, and exit code"
      - "Run: memory_health({reportMode: 'full'})"
      - "Inspect memory_health.data.exclusionAudit; classify hard_exclusion_risk from diagnostics without changing recall"
      - "Run: memory_stats({})"
      - "Bash: stat -f '%m %z' database/context-index__*.sqlite (mtime + size for present profile DBs)"
      - "Interactive gate: before_phase_1_analysis"
    outputs:
      - cli_availability: available|retryable-unavailable|failed
      - total_records: N
      - last_index_at: ISO_timestamp
      - fts_health: ok|degraded|missing
      - embedding_health: ok|degraded|missing
      - profile_db_sizes: [{path: string, size: bytes}]
      - profile_db_mtimes: [{path: string, mtime: epoch_seconds}]
    validation: discovery_complete
    fail_fast:
      - "memory_health returns error → STATUS=FAIL with diagnostic"
      - "Database file missing → STATUS=DEGRADED, message='index not initialized'"

  phase_1_analysis:
    purpose: Classify drift signals + identify suspect records
    activities:
      - "Cross-reference memory_health output with mtime: any spec-doc file with mtime > last_index_at signals staleness"
      - "Sample up to 5 records flagged as drift candidates"
      - "Run memory_drift_why per sampled record (read-only)"
      - "Aggregate drift signals into drift_classes count map"
      - "Interactive gate: before_phase_2_recommendation"
    outputs:
      - drift_classes:
          embedding_drift: N
          fts_miss: M
          chunk_boundary_drift: K
          trigger_phrase_drift: L

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """
3: route-validate.py — Canonical-manifest CI assertion for /doctor router.
4: 
5: Validates `.opencode/commands/doctor/_routes.yaml` against:
6:   A. YAML parse + schema_version
7:   B. Routes list integrity + required keys per route
8:   C. No duplicate target names
9:   D. Every route's YAML asset exists in assets/
10:   E. Mutation class is one of {read-only, add-only, mutates}
11:   F. Each route's mcp_tools is a subset of the router's frontmatter allowed-tools union
12:   G. Every route has ≥1 trigger phrase
13:   H. Flag-name collisions across targets (informational only)
14: 
15: Exit codes:
16:   0 — all assertions pass
17:   1 — at least one assertion failure
18:   2 — manifest missing or unparseable
19: """
20: from __future__ import annotations
21: 
22: import argparse
23: import os
24: import re
25: import sys
26: from pathlib import Path
27: 
28: try:
29:     import yaml
30: except ImportError:
31:     print("ERROR: PyYAML required. Install via: pip3 install pyyaml", file=sys.stderr)
32:     sys.exit(3)
33: 
34: 
35: REQUIRED_KEYS = {
36:     "target",
37:     "yaml",
38:     "setup_vars",
39:     "allowed_flags",
40:     "mutating",
41:     "gate3_location",
42:     "mcp_tools",
43:     "trigger_phrases",
44: }
45: VALID_MUTATING = {"read-only", "add-only", "mutates"}
46: 
47: # ANSI colors (skip if not a TTY)
48: IS_TTY = sys.stdout.isatty()
49: def color(text: str, code: str) -> str:
50:     if not IS_TTY:
51:         return text
52:     return f"\033[{code}m{text}\033[0m"
53: 
54: def red(s):    return color(s, "31")
55: def green(s):  return color(s, "32")
56: def yellow(s): return color(s, "33")
57: def blue(s):   return color(s, "34")
58: 
59: 
60: class Result:
61:     def __init__(self):
62:         self.fails = 0
63:         self.warns = 0
64: 
65:     def fail(self, msg: str):
66:         print(f"{red('FAIL')}: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
67:         self.fails += 1
68: 
69:     def warn(self, msg: str):
70:         print(f"{yellow('WARN')}: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
71:         self.warns += 1
72: 
73:     def passed(self, msg: str):
74:         print(f"{green('PASS')}: {msg}")
75: 
76:     def info(self, msg: str):
77:         print(f"{blue('INFO')}: {msg}")
78: 
79: 
80: def parse_router_allowed_tools(router_path: Path) -> set[str]:
81:     """Extract allowed-tools list from the router .md frontmatter."""
82:     if not router_path.exists():
83:         return set()
84:     text = router_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
85:     # Find first '---' frontmatter block
86:     match = re.search(r"^---\n(.*?)\n---\n", text, re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE)
87:     if not match:
88:         return set()
89:     fm_text = match.group(1)
90:     # Find the allowed-tools line (may span multiple physical lines if YAML-folded;
91:     # current format is a single comma-separated line)
92:     at_match = re.search(r"^allowed-tools:\s*(.+?)(?=\n\w|\Z)", fm_text, re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE)
93:     if not at_match:
94:         return set()
95:     raw = at_match.group(1).strip()
96:     # Normalize: split on commas, strip whitespace
97:     tools = {t.strip() for t in raw.split(",") if t.strip()}
98:     return tools
99: 
100: 
101: def main():
102:     ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
103:     ap.add_argument("--routes", required=True, help="Path to _routes.yaml")
104:     ap.add_argument("--router", required=True, help="Path to doctor.md (router)")
105:     ap.add_argument("--assets-dir", required=True, help="Path to assets/ dir")
106:     args = ap.parse_args()
107: 
108:     routes_path = Path(args.routes)
109:     router_path = Path(args.router)

116:     R.info(f"Assets:   {assets_dir}")
117:     print("")
118: 
119:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120:     # A. MANIFEST PARSE + SCHEMA VERSION
121:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122:     if not routes_path.exists():
123:         print(f"{red('ERROR')}: manifest not found at {routes_path}", file=sys.stderr)
124:         return 2
125: 
126:     try:
127:         with routes_path.open() as f:
128:             manifest = yaml.safe_load(f)
129:     except yaml.YAMLError as e:
130:         print(f"{red('ERROR')}: manifest parse error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
131:         return 2
132: 
133:     R.passed("A1: manifest parses as YAML")
134: 
135:     schema_version = manifest.get("schema_version")
136:     if schema_version != 1:
137:         R.fail(f"A2: schema_version is {schema_version!r}; expected 1")
138:     else:
139:         R.passed("A2: schema_version is 1")
140: 
141:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
142:     # B. ROUTES LIST INTEGRITY
143:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

147:         return 1 if R.fails else 0
148: 
149:     R.passed(f"B1: .routes has {len(routes)} entries")
150: 
151:     for i, route in enumerate(routes):

154:             continue

156:         missing = REQUIRED_KEYS - set(route.keys())

160:     if R.fails == 0:

162: 
163:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164:     # C. DUPLICATE TARGET CHECK
165:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
166:     targets = [r.get("target") for r in routes if isinstance(r, dict)]
167:     seen = set()
168:     dupes = set()
169:     for t in targets:

171:             dupes.add(t)
172:         seen.add(t)
173:     if dupes:
174:         R.fail(f"C1: duplicate target names: {', '.join(sorted(dupes))}")
175:     else:
176:         R.passed("C1: no duplicate target names")
177: 
178:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
179:     # D. YAML ASSET EXISTENCE
180:     # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
181:     missing_assets = []
182:     for route in routes:
183:         if not isinstance(route, dict):
184:             continue
185:         target = route.get("target")
186:         yaml_name = route.get("yaml")
187:         if not yaml_name:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
route-validate.py — Canonical-manifest CI assertion for /doctor router.

Validates `.opencode/commands/doctor/_routes.yaml` against:
  A. YAML parse + schema_version
  B. Routes list integrity + required keys per route
  C. No duplicate target names
  D. Every route's YAML asset exists in assets/
  E. Mutation class is one of {read-only, add-only, mutates}
  F. Each route's mcp_tools is a subset of the router's frontmatter allowed-tools union
  G. Every route has ≥1 trigger phrase
  H. Flag-name collisions across targets (informational only)

Exit codes:
  0 — all assertions pass
  1 — at least one assertion failure
  2 — manifest missing or unparseable
"""
from __future__ import annotations
